overall_summary: Development focused on CI/CD optimization and core agent orchestration refinements while the community reported significant platform access barriers and persistent UI bugs on Windows. The launch of 'Eliza Cloud Apps' marks a strategic shift toward modular infrastructure, despite unresolved tenant-level invitation errors and multi-chain bridging inquiries.
key_facts: PR #8408 was merged on June 14 to implement path-based gating for expensive test lanes in the eliza repository.
Multiple users reported a persistent blank screen error on Windows when running 'bun run dev:ui' despite successful agent synchronization.
Eliza Cloud access is currently blocked for several users due to a tenant 'elizacloud' invitation requirement error.
Odilitime confirmed that the Babylon platform is currently on hold with plans to revive social media components after internal discussions.
PR #8277 decomposed the 'plugin-lifeops' monolith into six specialized plugins including plugin-relationships and plugin-finances.
A strategic proposal was submitted to integrate elizaOS with over 3 million devices on the peaq network via a native plugin.
Development work on June 14 added acknowledgment progress mode and fixed sub-agent routing logic for app-build spawns.
GitHub activity for the Milady project has been noted as silent with no recent commits visible to the community.
open_questions: How do I fix the white/blank screen when running 'bun run dev:ui' on Windows?
What is the best way to bridge elizaOS from BNB to Solana?
Any Milady update? Github is silent
When will the new world models tooling be available?

new_issues_prs.title: ci: gate expensive test lanes by changed paths
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Reduces CI/CD overhead by skipping unnecessary test execution for server, client, and plugin workflows.

new_issues_prs.title: refactor(lifeops): split plugin-lifeops into focused plugins + plugin-personal-assistant
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Major architectural refactor of a 7.5K-LOC monolith into modular, specialized components.

new_issues_prs.title: Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical audit identifying P0 blockers including missing ingress and Postgres connection ceilings.

overall_focus.claim: Development is centered on modularizing the core architecture and automating cloud infrastructure to support multi-tenant hosting.

discord_updates.summary: Technical support requests dominated the channel, focusing on Eliza Cloud access errors and UI rendering bugs on Windows. Users also sought updates on Babylon and Milady project status.
The channel lacked technical substance during this period, containing unrelated partnership inquiries and solicitations for purchasing crypto wallets.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed frustration over the inability to join Eliza Cloud due to 'tenant invitation' errors and a lack of documentation on how to acquire such invitations.
Community concern is rising regarding the silence on the Milady GitHub repository, with users questioning the project's current development status.

strategic_insights.insight: Persistent local development UI bugs on Windows and cloud onboarding friction (invitation errors) are creating barriers to developer adoption.
implications_or_questions: Should the team prioritize a Windows-specific stability patch?
How can the cloud onboarding flow be simplified to avoid invitation deadlocks?

strategic_insights.insight: The proposed peaq integration represents a significant opportunity to move elizaOS into the DePIN and hardware robotics sectors.
implications_or_questions: Does this align with the framework's primary focus on digital AI agents?

market_analysis.observation: Strategic shift toward Decentralized AI backends as an alternative to OpenAI/Google through Akash and peaq integrations.
market_analysis.relevance: Positions elizaOS as a key player in the 'DeAI' and decentralized machine economy.
